title: MCP Security, Prompt Injection, and Supply Chain Trust · Integration Triage
summary: Threat-model an MCP server consuming untrusted content and exposing write tools. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in an MCP security test suite and operating policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect server provenance, dependencies, update channel, permissions, and data destinations.
Treat external resource and tool content as untrusted data that cannot redefine authority.
Bind actions to user intent, authorization, audience, and protected resource.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
Use a changed debug case derived from this module: agent ext mcp security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Server Trust trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Security Operations applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Patch, rotate, revoke, audit, rate-limit, and incident-test MCP deployments.
